TermsOfUsePage.title: Terms of Use
TermsOfUsePage.intro: Verdict is a free, open-source movie discovery app built to help you find something worth watching — any mood, any group, any evening. These Terms of Use ("Terms") govern your access to and use of Verdict ("Service"). Please take a moment to read them before getting started.

sections.acceptanceBody: By accessing, installing, or using the Service in any way, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, please discontinue use of the Service immediately.
sections.eligibilityBody: You must be at least 13 years of age (or the minimum age required in your jurisdiction) to use the Service. You are solely responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for all activity that occurs under your account, whether through email/password or supported providers such as Google OAuth. Any unauthorized use of your account should be reported to phexuss@icloud.com immediately.
sections.serviceBody: Verdict provides movie discovery and AI-assisted recommendation features. The Service integrates third-party data sources and APIs, including The Movie Database (TMDB). Verdict does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or availability of any third-party content.
sections.tmdbBody: This product uses the TMDB API but is not endorsed or certified by TMDB. All movie metadata, images, ratings, and related content sourced from TMDB remain the property of their respective owners and are subject to TMDB's Terms of Use and API policies.
sections.acceptableUseBody: You agree not to: (a) use the Service for any unlawful purpose or in violation of applicable law; (b) attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Service or its infrastructure; (c) interfere with or disrupt the integrity or performance of the Service; (d) scrape or harvest data in violation of any API or provider terms; (e) impersonate any person or entity.
sections.openSourceBody: The Verdict source code is available under the open-source license specified in the project repository. Rights to use, copy, modify, and distribute the source code are governed exclusively by that license. These Terms do not grant any rights to the source code beyond what that license provides.
sections.ipBody: Source code is owned by its respective copyright holders under the applicable open-source license. All third-party trademarks, service marks, logos, and content — including TMDB branding and movie assets — are the property of their respective owners. No third-party intellectual property rights are transferred to you by virtue of using the Service.
sections.availabilityBody: The Service is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. Verdict reserves the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any part of the Service at any time and without prior notice.
sections.disclaimerBody: To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, the Service is provided without warranties of any kind — express, implied, or statutory — including warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, or uninterrupted and error-free operation.
sections.liabilityBody: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Verdict and its contributors shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages — including loss of data, revenue, profits, or goodwill — arising from your use of or inability to use the Service, regardless of the theory of liability.
sections.terminationBody: Verdict reserves the right to suspend or terminate your access at any time for conduct that violates these Terms or applicable law, or that is harmful to other users or the integrity of the Service. Upon termination, all rights and licenses granted under these Terms cease immediately.
sections.changesBody: These Terms may be updated from time to time. Continued use of the Service after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms. It is worth checking this page occasionally.
sections.contactBody: For any questions or concerns about these Terms, reach out at phexuss@icloud.com.

PrivacyPolicyPage.title: Privacy Policy
PrivacyPolicyPage.intro: Verdict is built to handle your data responsibly and transparently. This Privacy Policy explains what information Verdict collects, how it is used, and what choices are available to you.

sections.dataWeCollectBody: Depending on how the Service is used, Verdict may process: (a) Account Data — email address, display name, and authentication identifiers provided during registration or via a third-party provider; (b) Preference and Activity Data — movies saved, watched, rated, disliked, or otherwise interacted with; (c) Technical Data — session tokens, cookies, device type, browser metadata, and IP address, collected to operate and secure the Service.
sections.howWeUseBody: Verdict processes data to: (a) create and manage accounts and verify identity; (b) maintain active sessions and protect account endpoints; (c) store movie preferences and improve recommendation quality; (d) ensure the security and reliability of the Service; and (e) fulfill applicable legal obligations.
sections.authBody: Verdict uses a Better Auth-based authentication flow and may offer sign-in via Google OAuth. Session cookies or tokens are issued upon login to keep you signed in and verify your identity on protected endpoints. Tokens expire according to the configured session duration.
sections.tmdbBody: Verdict retrieves movie metadata, images, and related content from The Movie Database (TMDB) API. TMDB processes request data under its own privacy terms. Verdict is not responsible for the data practices of TMDB or any other third-party provider.
sections.sharingBody: Verdict does not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. Data may be disclosed only: (a) to infrastructure or service providers strictly necessary to operate the Service, bound by confidentiality obligations; or (b) when required by law, court order, or governmental authority.
sections.retentionBody: Verdict retains data for as long as an account is active and as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Policy, comply with legal obligations, and resolve disputes. Account deletion can be requested at phexuss@icloud.com.
sections.securityBody: Verdict implements reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. No internet-based system is completely secure, and absolute security cannot be guaranteed.
sections.childrenBody: The Service is not intended for individuals under 13 years of age, or the applicable minimum age in your jurisdiction. Verdict does not knowingly collect data from children. If such data is identified, steps will be taken to delete it promptly.
sections.rightsBody: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to the processing of your personal data, as well as the right to data portability. To exercise any of these rights, contact phexuss@icloud.com.
sections.internationalBody: Your data may be transferred to and processed in countries other than your own, where data protection laws may differ. By using the Service, you consent to such transfers to the extent permitted by applicable law.
sections.changesBody: This Privacy Policy may be revised periodically. The effective date at the top reflects the latest revision. Continued use of the Service after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy.
sections.contactBody: For privacy questions, data requests, or deletion inquiries, contact phexuss@icloud.com.

WelcomePage.title: <chunk>The last thing</chunk> <chunk>you'll scroll</chunk> <chunk><accent>tonight.</accent></chunk>
WelcomePage.description: Not sure what to watch? Verdict figures it out for you. Tell it your mood, who you're watching with, or what kind of evening it is — and it'll suggest something actually worth your time. No endless scrolling, no decision fatigue.
